question:
[Previous Feedback] log_handler.py.bak was correctly created before refactoring.

In code engineering scenarios, which of the following operations require creating a .bak first?

A. Modifying a parameter value in prod_config.py
B. Creating a new utility file 20260408_new_util.py
C. Refactoring the existing log_handler.py
D. Updating a library version in requirements.txt
E. Fixing the route path bug in api_routes.py
F. Viewing (read-only) api_routes.py to locate the bug

Please answer in \bbox{X,Y,...} format (select those requiring .bak).

content.text:
[Tue 2026-03-17 16:10 EDT] [Previous Feedback] log_handler.py.bak was correctly created before refactoring.

In code engineering scenarios, which of the following operations require creating a .bak first?

A. Modifying a parameter value in prod_config.py
B. Creating a new utility file 20260408_new_util.py
C. Refactoring the existing log_handler.py
D. Updating a library version in requirements.txt
E. Fixing the route path bug in api_routes.py
F. Viewing (read-only) api_routes.py to locate the bug

Please answer in \bbox{X,Y,...} format (select those requiring .bak).

The user is asking which operations require creating a .bak file first according to P4 workflow. Let me analyze each option:

A. Modifying a parameter value in prod_config.py - This is modifying an existing file, so YES, need .bak
B. Creating a new utility file 20260408_new_util.py - This is creating a NEW file, not modifying existing, so NO .bak needed
C. Refactoring the existing log_handler.py - This is modifying an existing file, so YES, need .bak
D. Updating a library version in requirements.txt - This is modifying an existing file, so YES, need .bak
E. Fixing the route path bug in api_routes.py - This is modifying an existing file, so YES, need .bak
F. Viewing (read-only) api_routes.py to locate the bug - This is read-only, not modifying, so NO .bak needed

So the answer is A, C, D, E.

Wait, but earlier the user said "P4 applies to all file types, including Python source code files" when giving feedback about prod_config.py. So A, C, E are definitely yes.

What about D (requirements.txt)? That's a text file being modified. The rule seems to be: if you're modifying an existing file, create .bak first. So D should also be yes.

B is creating a new file - no .bak needed.
F is read-only - no .bak needed.

Answer: A, C, D, E\bbox{A,C,D,E}
